roles.title: IT Service Desk Assistant
roles.description: First point of contact for IT queries, logging and triaging tickets, providing basic user support.

roles.title: Digital Health Stock & Equipment Assistant
roles.description: Manages inventory, procurement requests, and distribution of digital hardware and devices across a Trust.

roles.title: ICT Admin Support Officer
roles.description: Provides administrative support to the ICT department, managing schedules, documentation, and supplier correspondence.

roles.title: Digital Smartcard / RA Administrator
roles.description: Issues and manages NHS Smartcards and Role Based Access Controls (RBAC) for clinical and non-clinical staff.

roles.title: IT Hardware Technician (1st Line)
roles.description: Installs, configures and maintains desktop hardware, laptops, printers and peripherals across Trust sites.

roles.title: IT Service Desk Analyst
roles.description: Provides 1st/2nd line IT support to NHS staff via phone, portal and remote access. Logs, categorises and resolves incidents within SLA.

roles.title: 2nd Line IT Support Technician
roles.description: Handles escalated incidents from 1st line, provides in-depth diagnosis and resolution of hardware, software and network issues.

roles.title: ICT Field Engineer
roles.description: Provides on-site IT support across multiple NHS locations, deploying and maintaining hardware and network infrastructure.

roles.title: Digital Workspace Technician (M365 / Azure AD)
roles.description: Manages and supports the Microsoft 365 and Azure AD environment, device management via Intune, and digital workspace tooling.

roles.title: GP IT Systems Technician (EMIS / SystmOne)
roles.description: Supports and maintains primary care IT systems (EMIS Web, SystmOne) across GP practices within an ICB or CSU.

roles.title: 2nd/3rd Line IT Support Engineer
roles.description: Provides advanced technical support, handling complex escalations, infrastructure changes and project delivery support.

roles.title: Network Engineer (NHS / HSCN)
roles.description: Designs, implements and maintains NHS network infrastructure including HSCN connectivity, switches, routers and firewalls.

roles.title: Clinical Systems Support Analyst
roles.description: Supports clinical staff in the day-to-day use of clinical systems, resolving application issues and escalating to the development team.

roles.title: EPR Application Support Analyst
roles.description: Provides application-level support for EPR systems (Epic, Cerner, Lorenzo), including configuration, testing and user support.

roles.title: Cyber Security Analyst
roles.description: Monitors, detects and responds to cyber threats across the NHS network, supporting DSP Toolkit compliance and security incident management.

roles.title: Cloud Infrastructure Engineer
roles.description: Builds and manages cloud infrastructure on Azure/AWS for NHS organisations, including virtual machines, storage, networking and security.

roles.title: Systems Integration Analyst
roles.description: Analyses, designs and supports integrations between NHS clinical and business systems using HL7, FHIR and API-based messaging.

roles.title: IT Service Delivery Coordinator
roles.description: Coordinates IT service delivery across a Trust, managing SLAs, supplier relationships and escalations between teams.

roles.title: Cyber Security Risk Analyst
roles.description: Assesses and manages cyber security risks across NHS digital systems, supporting DSP Toolkit compliance and risk register maintenance.

roles.title: ICT Infrastructure Support Lead
roles.description: Leads a team of infrastructure engineers, overseeing day-to-day operations, escalations and technical delivery of infrastructure projects.

data.label: Health Data & Analytics
data.workPreferenceMatch: Working with data and turning it into insights

roles.title: Data Quality Administrator
roles.description: Supports the data quality team by identifying, reporting and correcting data errors in NHS clinical and administrative systems.

roles.title: Information & Records Assistant
roles.description: Maintains health records, supports information requests and assists with data filing, retrieval and information governance processes.

roles.title: Clinical Coding Assistant / Trainee Clinical Coder
roles.description: Trains to translate clinical information from patient records into coded data using ICD-10 and OPCS-4 classifications under supervision.

roles.title: Research Data Administrator
roles.description: Supports research teams with data collection, storage, cleaning and reporting, ensuring compliance with information governance standards.

roles.title: Data Solutions Administrator
roles.description: Administers data platforms and reporting tools, supporting the maintenance and development of NHS data solutions and dashboards.

roles.title: Information Assistant
roles.description: Collects, validates and presents data to support performance reporting and operational decision-making within an NHS organisation.

roles.title: Assistant Information Analyst
roles.description: Supports senior analysts in producing reports and dashboards, extracting and validating data from NHS systems.

roles.title: Data Quality Officer
roles.description: Identifies, investigates and resolves data quality issues in NHS systems, producing reports and supporting improvement plans.

roles.title: Clinical Coder (Qualified / ACC)
roles.description: Translates complex inpatient clinical information into coded data using ICD-10 and OPCS-4, ensuring accuracy for NHS tariff and reporting.

roles.title: Performance & Reporting Assistant
roles.description: Assists in producing regular performance reports and dashboards to support operational and board-level decision-making.

roles.title: Information Analyst
roles.description: Extracts, analyses and presents NHS data to support clinical and operational performance, producing dashboards and ad-hoc reports.

roles.title: Data Analyst (Health Informatics)
roles.description: Analyses complex health datasets to generate actionable insights, building dashboards and models to support NHS decision-making.

roles.title: Business Intelligence Analyst
roles.description: Develops and maintains BI solutions and dashboards across NHS systems, translating analytical requirements into reporting products.

roles.title: Data Warehouse Developer
roles.description: Designs, builds and maintains NHS data warehouse solutions, creating ETL pipelines and ensuring data quality and availability.

roles.title: Integration & Interoperability Analyst
roles.description: Develops and supports data integration between NHS clinical systems using HL7 and FHIR standards to enable interoperability.

roles.title: Health Informatics Graduate Trainee
roles.description: Structured rotational graduate programme covering data, informatics and analytics roles across NHS organisations.

roles.title: Population Health Analyst
roles.description: Analyses population-level health data to identify trends, inequalities and opportunities for preventive intervention within an ICS.

roles.title: Analytics Engineer
roles.description: Transforms and models NHS data for analytical consumption, building data pipelines and semantic layers that serve BI and reporting needs.

roles.title: Data Engineer (NHS)
roles.description: Builds scalable NHS data infrastructure, ingesting and transforming data from clinical systems into platforms for analytics and AI.

roles.title: Information Governance Officer
roles.description: Manages NHS information governance obligations including DSP Toolkit compliance, FOI/SAR responses and data protection impact assessments.

leadership.label: Leadership & Strategy
leadership.workPreferenceMatch: Leading projects and working with people

roles.title: Digital Project Administration Assistant
roles.description: Provides administrative support to digital project teams, including scheduling, document control and meeting coordination.

roles.title: Programme Support Administrator (Digital)
roles.description: Supports a digital programme management office with administrative tasks, risk logs, action tracking and reporting.

roles.title: Digital Communications & Admin Assistant
roles.description: Supports digital communications functions including intranet updates, staff newsletters and internal digital engagement campaigns.

roles.title: Digital Project Support Officer
roles.description: Provides structured support to digital project managers, maintaining project documentation, RAID logs and progress tracking.

roles.title: Programme Support Officer
roles.description: Manages programme-level administrative and reporting functions, supporting delivery leads with governance and planning.

roles.title: Digital Delivery Officer
roles.description: Supports agile delivery teams in coordinating sprint ceremonies, tracking deliverables and removing blockers in digital programmes.

roles.title: Benefits Realisation Support Officer
roles.description: Supports the identification, tracking and reporting of benefits delivered through NHS digital programmes and system implementations.

roles.title: Service Improvement Support Officer (Digital)
roles.description: Works within digital and operational teams to identify service improvement opportunities, supporting pathway redesign and QI projects.

roles.title: Digital Project Coordinator
roles.description: Coordinates digital projects end-to-end, managing plans, risks, resources and stakeholder communications within an NHS setting.

roles.title: Associate / Assistant Digital Product Manager
roles.description: Supports a senior product manager in defining and delivering digital health products, gathering requirements and managing backlog.

roles.title: Business Analyst (Digital Health)
roles.description: Investigates and documents business requirements for NHS digital solutions, mapping processes and translating needs into system specifications.

roles.title: Digital Change & Adoption Officer
roles.description: Drives user adoption of digital systems and tools, designing engagement strategies and supporting staff through technology change.

roles.title: Agile Delivery Officer / Scrum Master
roles.description: Facilitates agile ceremonies, removes delivery blockers and coaches NHS teams to improve velocity and delivery outcomes.

roles.title: Digital Transformation Coordinator
roles.description: Coordinates workstreams within NHS digital transformation programmes, supporting delivery, stakeholder engagement and reporting.

roles.title: IT Project Manager
roles.description: Manages NHS IT projects end-to-end, leading multi-disciplinary teams to deliver digital infrastructure and system implementation projects.

roles.title: Digital Communications Officer
roles.description: Develops and delivers NHS digital communications strategies, managing intranet content, staff engagement and digital campaign delivery.

roles.title: Service Improvement Coordinator (Digital)
roles.description: Leads service improvement initiatives within digital programmes, applying QI methodology to redesign pathways and optimise system use.

roles.title: User Researcher (Digital Health)
roles.description: Conducts user research to inform the design and development of NHS digital services, using interviews, usability testing and contextual inquiry.

clinical.label: Clinical Practice & Informatics
clinical.workPreferenceMatch: Being close to patient care and clinical workflows

roles.title: Clinical Systems Administration Assistant
roles.description: Provides administrative support to clinical systems teams, managing user access requests, system documentation and helpdesk queries.

roles.title: EPR Data Entry & Support Assistant
roles.description: Supports clinical teams by entering, validating and maintaining patient data in EPR systems to ensure record accuracy.

roles.title: Digital Clinical Admin Support Officer
roles.description: Supports the clinical digital team with admin functions including scheduling, reporting, documentation and stakeholder communications.

roles.title: Clinical Systems Support Technician
roles.description: Provides technical support for clinical systems at ward and departmental level, triaging issues and liaising with application teams.

roles.title: ePrescribing / Pharmacy Systems Assistant
roles.description: Supports the implementation and day-to-day operation of electronic prescribing systems, assisting clinical staff with system use and queries.

roles.title: Radiology / PACS Support Assistant
roles.description: Supports the radiology IT team with PACS/RIS administration, image management and user support for imaging system queries.

roles.title: Clinical Coding Support Officer
roles.description: Assists accredited clinical coders with coding preparation, data validation and departmental administration.

roles.title: Digital Clinical Champion
roles.description: Acts as a peer advocate for digital tools within a clinical team, supporting colleagues in adopting new systems and championing digital working.

roles.title: Clinical Systems Analyst
roles.description: Analyses clinical system performance and user needs, supporting configuration, testing and optimisation of NHS EPR environments.

roles.title: EPR Clinical Informatics Analyst
roles.description: Works within EPR implementation and optimisation programmes, translating clinical workflows into system build requirements.

roles.title: Digital Change Partner (Clinical)
roles.description: Embedded within clinical divisions to lead change and adoption activity around digital systems, supporting staff through transformation.

roles.title: ePrescribing Systems Analyst
roles.description: Develops, configures and supports electronic prescribing and medicines administration (EPMA) systems across inpatient and outpatient settings.

roles.title: Nurse Informaticist
roles.description: Bridges nursing practice and digital systems, advising on EPR design, supporting implementation and improving clinical data quality.

roles.title: Digital Lead Nurse / Midwife
roles.description: Leads digital nursing or midwifery informatics within a Trust, driving safe and effective EPR use, clinical safety and digital strategy.

roles.title: Pharmacy Systems Analyst
roles.description: Manages and develops pharmacy IT systems including JAC, EPMA and dispensing systems, supporting optimisation and integration.

roles.title: Radiology / PACS Analyst
roles.description: Supports, configures and develops radiology information systems (RIS) and PACS platforms, ensuring imaging workflow efficiency.

roles.title: Clinical Safety Analyst (DCB0129)
roles.description: Ensures NHS digital systems meet clinical safety standards (DCB0129), producing hazard logs, safety cases and clinical risk management documentation.

roles.title: Allied Health Professional (AHP) Informatics Lead
roles.description: Leads digital informatics for AHP services (physiotherapy, OT, dietetics etc.), ensuring EPR systems reflect AHP clinical workflows.

roles.title: GP Clinical Systems Trainer
roles.description: Delivers training and ongoing support to GP practice staff on EMIS, SystmOne and GP Connect digital systems.

roles.title: Digital Medicines Technician
roles.description: Supports digital medicines management, including ePrescribing, electronic stock management and pharmacy system optimisation.

education.label: Education & Training
education.workPreferenceMatch: Teaching, training and building people's capability

roles.title: IT Training Administrator
roles.description: Provides administrative support to the digital training team, scheduling courses, managing bookings and maintaining attendance records.

roles.title: eLearning Content & Admin Assistant
roles.description: Assists the digital education team in uploading, maintaining and administering eLearning content on the Trust's LMS platform.

roles.title: Digital Training Bookings & Scheduling Officer
roles.description: Manages the scheduling and administration of digital training programmes, co-ordinating bookings and communications for NHS staff.

roles.title: LMS (Learning Management System) Administrator
roles.description: Manages the Trust's LMS platform (e.g. ESR, Oracle Learn), administering user accounts, course content, compliance reports and system configuration.

roles.title: Digital Skills Development Officer
certifications: Level 3 Award in Education and Training (AET)
roles.description: Designs and delivers digital skills development programmes to improve the digital literacy of NHS staff across the organisation.

roles.title: Digital Adoption Support Officer
roles.description: Provides on-the-floor support to NHS staff during digital system rollouts, coaching individuals to build confidence and competence.

roles.title: EPR Training Support Officer
roles.description: Assists the EPR training team with scheduling, materials preparation, training environment testing and attendance record keeping.

roles.title: Digital Champion Coordinator
roles.description: Coordinates a network of digital champions across a Trust, providing support, resources and recognition to peer advocates of digital tools.

roles.title: Digital Health Trainer
roles.description: Plans and delivers digital training programmes to NHS staff, covering EPR systems, Office 365, digital tools and clinical applications.

roles.title: Clinical Systems Trainer (Epic / Cerner / SystmOne)
roles.description: Delivers specialist training on a named EPR system (Epic, Cerner, SystmOne), including classroom delivery, go-live floorwalking and user assessment.

roles.title: eLearning Developer
roles.description: Creates interactive eLearning modules and digital learning resources using Articulate Storyline/Rise, supporting NHS staff education at scale.

roles.title: Digital Literacy Facilitator
roles.description: Delivers digital literacy programmes targeted at staff with low digital confidence, supporting the NHS workforce digital upskilling agenda.

roles.title: Digital Education & Training Officer
roles.description: Manages end-to-end digital education delivery, commissioning and evaluating training programmes to meet the Trust's digital capability needs.

roles.title: Workforce Digital Capability Assessor
roles.description: Assesses digital competency levels across the NHS workforce, designing targeted interventions to address skills gaps and support appraisal frameworks.

roles.title: Technology Enhanced Learning (TEL) Officer
roles.description: Leads the development and management of technology-enhanced learning solutions, including virtual classrooms, videos and simulation tools.

roles.title: Instructional Designer (Healthcare)
roles.description: Designs and develops instructional content for NHS digital learning programmes, applying adult learning principles and SCORM-based standards.

roles.title: Training Needs Analyst (Digital)
roles.description: Conducts organisational training needs analyses for digital programmes, mapping capability gaps to structured learning interventions.

roles.title: Digital Health Coach
roles.description: Provides 1-2-1 and group coaching to NHS staff and teams to build digital confidence, resilience and capability in adopting new technologies.

backgroundOptions.label: I'm a practising clinician (nurse, doctor, AHP, pharmacist, etc.)
I work in NHS/healthcare but not in a clinical role
I work in tech/IT outside of healthcare
I'm a student or recent graduate
I'm changing careers from a non-tech, non-clinical background

clinicalIntentOptions.label: Stay close to clinical work but add a digital dimension
Move fully into a digital/informatics role and leave clinical practice
I'm not sure yet — show me both options

experienceOptions.label: 5+ years in a non-digital role (career changer — treat as entry for digital)

workPreferenceOptions.label: Working with data and turning it into insights
Leading projects and working with people
Being close to patient care and clinical workflows
Teaching, training and building people's capability
